frameworks.name: efficient coding
frameworks.one_line: Contextual modulation aligns encoding with natural-scene orientation statistics.

frameworks.name: Fisher information encoding-decoding
frameworks.one_line: Links behavioral bias/variance and neural population FI to a shared encoding accuracy.

frameworks.name: voxel-wise probabilistic encoding model
frameworks.one_line: Used to estimate population Fisher information from fMRI in retinotopic areas.

core_question: Does surround context shape orientation encoding behaviorally and neurally in a way consistent with efficient coding of natural scene statistics?
key_assumptions: Bias and variance in orientation reports lawfully relate to Fisher information.
Voxel responses can be modeled with probabilistic orientation encoders.
Natural-scene orientation statistics (marginal and surround-conditional) are the relevant prior.
manipulations: Surround type: unoriented noise vs oriented gratings.
Surround orientation: two fixed oriented surrounds (±30° from vertical).
Visual area along the ventral hierarchy.
key_findings: With unoriented surround, behavioral and neural FI track marginal natural-scene orientation statistics.
Oriented surrounds increase encoding accuracy at the surround orientation, matching conditional scene statistics.
Surround modulation of FI grows progressively along the ventral visual hierarchy.
interpretations: Contextual surround modulation implements a form of efficient sensory coding.
Behavior and neural population activity reflect a common context-dependent encoding scheme.
